title: glaucium corniculatum subsp. refractum
intro: Glaucium corniculatum subsp. refractum, commonly known as the lesser corn poppy or red poppy, is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family Papaveraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, as well as its ability to act as a natural pain reliever. Consuming the plant has been associated with several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, as well as aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Glaucium corniculatum subsp. refractum include tinctures, infusions, and dried flowers, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
